name: Chicken-And-Vegetable Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 large Leeks; green tops and white bottoms separated
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the boiling chickens and water in a 5-quart stockpot. Cover, bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low and skim off the scum that accumulates on the surface.
2. Remove chicken breast quarters and set aside.
3. Add 2 cups carrots, 1 cup onions, 2 cups celery, green leek tops, peppercorns, bay leaves, thyme, and salt. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes.
4. Remove cover, replace the chicken breasts, and continue to cook for another 25 minutes.
5. Slice the white parts of leeks into 1/4-inch rounds and wash well to remove any sand (about 1 cup). Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat and add the leeks and remaining vegetables. Cover and cook gently until vegetables are soft, about 5 minutes.
6. Remove chicken from the liquid and place on a platter. Strain the liquid through a fine strainer into a large plastic container and discard herbs, spices, and cooked soup vegetables. Skim and discard any fat from the surface of the broth. Remove and discard chicken skin. Dice the meat and set aside.
7. Place bones in an airtight freezer container for future stock.
8. Immediately prior to serving, transfer broth to a pot and heat, covered, over high heat. Heat chicken and vegetables in a 350°F oven until hot. Decorate each soup bowl with heated diced vegetables and chicken. Pour the hot broth into a pitcher or soup tureen and serve the soup in the garnished bowls at the table.
